# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Workflow

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Check out code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Extract PR information
        id: pr_data
        run: |
          PR_ID=$(echo $GITHUB_REF | awk 'BEGIN { FS = "/" } ; { print $3 }')
          PR_TITLE=$(gh pr view https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/pull/${PR_ID} --json title -q .title)
          PR_DESCRIPTION=$(gh pr view https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/pull/${PR_ID} --json body -q .body)
          echo "::set-output name=pr_title::$PR_TITLE"
          echo "::set-output name=pr_description::$PR_DESCRIPTION"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build and Test
        run: echo "Build and test runs here"

      - name: Commit message with PR title
        run: git commit -am "${{ steps.pr_data.outputs.pr_title }}"

      - name: Send Build Notifications
        uses: 8398a7/action-slack@v3
        with:
          status: ${{ job.status }}
          text: 'Build about PR "${{ steps.pr_data.outputs.pr_title }}" has "${{ job.status }}" status.'
          fields: repo,commit,action
          env:
            SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
